Issues: The aesthetic is highly derivative and falls into the 'synthwave/retrowave' genre, which is extremely overused and highly saturated in the current marketplace. This significantly lowers its perceived uniqueness.

While the letters 'QUEY' do not immediately appear to violate IP, if this sequence is intended to represent a specific brand name or acronym, the creator must verify trademark availability in all intended sales regions (T-shirt blanks, merchandise, digital use).
The composition, while aesthetically pleasing, relies entirely on established, recognizable visual clichés (neon gradients, grid lines, stylized palm trees). This makes it difficult to stand out.
For commercial appeal, the concept is inherently generic. To sell it successfully, it would require heavy branding or modification to tie it to a specific niche or audience.
